sourceMovie.title: Army of the Dead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Army of the Dead for the same reason it broke through in 2021: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Zack Snyder keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saw
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Army of the Dead clicked for you, Saw (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Army of the Dead clicked for you, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Army of the Dead?
faq.answer: Try Saw, Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Beverly Hills Cop—each shares DNA with Army of the Dead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Army of the Dead?
faq.answer: Army of the Dead (2021) is best described as Crime, Action, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Army of the Dead part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Army of the Dead Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
